title: Vetra Studio automatically generates code when specification documents change
content:
When using Vetra Studio with `ph vetra --watch`, code generation is fully automatic:

1. You create or modify specification documents in the Vetra Studio Drive browser UI
2. Vetra monitors changes in real-time
3. When a document model specification becomes valid, Vetra triggers code generation
4. Terminal shows: `[Vetra] Document model TodoList is valid, proceeding with code generation`
5. Validation errors are reported in the terminal to help identify missing specifications

This contrasts with the legacy Connect workflow where you must:
1. Export a `.phd` file from Connect
2. Manually run `ph generate TodoList.phd`

In **interactive mode** (`ph vetra --interactive`), Vetra prompts for confirmation before processing each change. In **standard mode**, changes are processed automatically with a 1-second debounce, batching multiple changes together.

The generated output is identical regardless of whether generation is automatic (Vetra) or manual (`ph generate`).
description: In watch mode, Vetra monitors specs and scaffolds directory/code in real-time, reporting validation errors in the terminal.
